spec_type_rationale: Upstream /discover; shell is net-new components/organisms/ tree + net-new settingsStore, no existing shell to modify

findings.content: Shared renderer state lives in a zustand store; never mutate state outside store actions; prefer selectors — settingsStore drag handlers commit via actions.
strict TS on, no 'any'; type external input as 'unknown' and narrow with a guard — settingsStore state + slot props fully typed.
zustand store camelCase + Store suffix → settingsStore.ts; React components PascalCase → Shell/Sidebar/PaneSplit/Titlebar/Statusbar.
Style via semantic class names bound to tokens.css custom properties, no inline styles; exported functions/types carry doc comments.
Renderer is sandboxed React UI with no Node/electron imports; persistence (electron-store) is a MAIN-process responsibility — renderer settingsStore stays in-memory this task.
Renderer UI lives at src/renderer/src; imports resolve via the @renderer alias, not deep relative paths.
Gates: npm run typecheck (node+web), npm run lint, npm run build (electron-vite), test suite — must stay green.
UI state held in zustand; shell is top-level renderer chrome; App.tsx is the renderer mount root.
Renderer has atoms + molecules + thin lib/ tiers; no organisms tier yet — the Shell tree introduces a net-new components/organisms/ tier.
toastStore is a single module-level zustand store mutated only via actions; settingsStore mirrors it (create() + selector hooks + reset for tests).
Semantic class names bound to tokens.css; per-component sibling .css file; no inline styles; animations gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
Renderer test stack: Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event (jsdom) for interaction; Playwright CT for real-browser focus/keyboard; tests under __tests__/.
App.tsx mounts ToastProvider+ToastViewport once and dev-gates PrimitivesDemo behind import.meta.env.DEV — the Shell organism mounts at this root.
App.tsx is the renderer's root component where the Shell mounts; single ToastProvider+ToastViewport lives here.
Renderer carries no Node/Electron imports — confirms shell + settingsStore are renderer-only/in-memory.
Design tokens are CSS custom properties in tokens.css; classNames composed with cx() — layout uses CSS vars + cx() BEM classes.

settingsStore is the SSOT { theme, accent, mstyle, sidebarWidth, paneRatio, sidebarCollapsed } + actions; Shell writes sidebarWidth/paneRatio to CSS custom properties on its root and sets data-theme/data-accent/data-mstyle on <html> via effect.

tokens.css ALREADY implements the [data-theme='dark'] override block and the [data-mstyle] method-style variants on <html>; shell EXTENDS by setting attributes. Attribute is data-mstyle, NOT data-method-style.

NO [data-accent] CSS block exists — accent is a single --accent var; data-accent is visually inert this task. Persist accent in the store but it drives no CSS; defining accent palettes is out of scope.

No settings store exists today (only toastStore); settingsStore is net-new mirroring toastStore, in-memory only. Disk persistence (electron-store/IPC) does not exist and is deferred to a later persistence-port task.

Hand-roll dividers: pointer-event components, rAF-batched CSS-var write during drag, commit px/ratio to store on release; clamp sidebar 200-520px and pane ratio 0.15-0.85 in JS; ARIA separator role + aria-valuenow + keyboard resize + drag-release-outside-window by hand. Library path rejected (owns layout state, violates SSOT); react-resizable-panels documented fallback.

Named mount slots sidebar/tabs/panes/modals accept arbitrary children and render them without the shell knowing their contents (CONTENTS out of scope); named React children/context (option A), not a slot-registry reducer.

Titlebar carries logo, workspace pill, sidebar toggle, command-palette trigger, environment selector, account pill; statusbar at bottom; Cmd-B toggles sidebar. App.tsx currently renders PrimitivesDemo — swap to the Shell organism.

Success: visual fidelity vs design/reference.html + design/tokens.json under design-auditor (look/behavior only, never markup); both dividers drag+clamp, Cmd-B toggles, view settings + width + ratio survive within session (component-tested); decoupled slots; gates green + zero reference-export cruft (no data-om-*, __OmT, inline styles, tweaks-panel).

Derisk: spike divider drag smoothness (rAF vs setState-per-move); prove clamp math on window-resize re-clamp (no negative/overflow panes); axe + keyboard/SR a11y pass on separators; mount the existing Tabs molecule into the tab slot to prove content-decoupling.

[NEEDS CLARIFICATION integration_points]: the user belief of persisting via an EXISTING settings store is wrong — the store is net-new + in-memory; disk persistence deferred. The single open uncertainty.
001 established renderer atoms/molecules/lib tiers + cx() + tokens.css token-bound styling — shell adds an organisms tier and reuses cx()/tokens.css.
001 installed the renderer test stack (Vitest + Testing Library + Playwright CT) recorded in docs/architecture.md — shell tests reuse it, no new test infra.
001 precedent: never reproduce design/reference.html markup or generated cruft (data-om-*, __OmT, inline styles, tweaks-panel) — same hygiene exclusion applies to the shell.
The existing Tabs molecule (controlled, selection-only) is the concrete real consumer to mount into the shell's tab slot to prove content-decoupling.
002 controlled-only precedent (caller owns state) — for the shell the settingsStore is the external state owner; slots stay mount-only.
002 noted request/response panes do not exist yet; the shell creates those pane mount slots (empty) — pane CONTENTS remain out of scope.
tokens.css has a [data-theme='dark'] override block and [data-mstyle] method-style variant blocks scoped to .method, all on <html>; no [data-accent] selector exists.
--accent:#10b981 plus --accent-soft/--accent-hover derived via color-mix; single accent var, not attribute-switched — store persists accent but no palette swap this task.
Tokens are CSS custom properties on :root — the Shell drives sidebarWidth/paneRatio through CSS vars on its own root during drag without per-move React re-render.
toastStore uses zustand create(), exports a single module-level instance, typed state + actions, no node/electron imports — settingsStore mirrors this shape.
toastStore exposes reactive selector usage — settingsStore exposes selector hooks for theme/accent/mstyle/sidebarWidth/paneRatio/sidebarCollapsed; mutate via actions only.
toastStore pattern includes a reset for tests — settingsStore provides the same so component tests start from known defaults.

decision_points.description: Accent: persist value but visually inert (no [data-accent] CSS) vs author accent palettes now
decision_points.user_answer: persist-inert: settingsStore holds accent + shell sets data-accent on <html>, but accent is visually inert this task (no [data-accent] CSS); accent palette CSS deferred to a later task.

decision_points.description: Resizable dividers: hand-roll pointer-event components vs adopt a panel library
decision_points.user_answer: hand-roll: zero-dep pointer-event dividers, rAF + CSS var during drag, clamp in JS, hand-written ARIA separator + keyboard resize; settingsStore is the single source of truth for width/ratio. react-resizable-panels documented as fallback.

decision_points.description: Cmd-B / sidebar-toggle semantics: collapse to hidden preserving width vs shrink to min
decision_points.user_answer: collapse-to-hidden: Cmd-B toggles a sidebarCollapsed boolean that hides the sidebar entirely; last sidebarWidth is preserved and restored on reopen.

decision_points.description: App.tsx dev-only PrimitivesDemo: replace with Shell vs keep reachable
valid_implementations: replace mount; keep PrimitivesDemo dev-gated but unmounted
mount PrimitivesDemo in a shell slot in DEV
decision_points.user_answer: replace, keep gated: the Shell organism becomes the App.tsx mount; PrimitivesDemo stays dev-gated (import.meta.env.DEV) but is no longer mounted at the root.
hardcode defaults aligned to tokens.css :root (light theme, mint accent, default method-style) and reference.html proportions; exact default values finalized in /plan.

decision_points.description: Window shrinks below clamp minimums: how layout re-clamps
valid_implementations: re-clamp width/ratio on resize to stay valid
decision_points.user_answer: re-clamp to stay valid: on window resize the shell re-clamps sidebarWidth to 200-520px and paneRatio to 0.15-0.85 so no pane goes negative or overflows; covered by a behavior test.

decision_points.description: no relevant decision point for breaking_changes
no relevant decision point for tooling_configuration

mandatory_reads.n_a_reason: constitution.md (Existing-Codebase mode) has §1-6 only — no Section 7 scaffolding guide; renderer scaffolding pattern is defined by the established atoms/molecules/lib tiers in docs/architecture.md + specs 001/002.

No novel external framework pattern to research — shell reuses internally-vetted patterns (zustand store mirroring toastStore, React tree, tokens.css contract); divider library options were vetted + rejected in the discover doc.
File does not exist in this repo; no prior-feature lessons file present.

overview: A single-window app shell and layout for the mintEnvoy renderer: a titlebar (logo, workspace pill, sidebar toggle, command-palette trigger, environment selector, account pill), a resizable left sidebar (width clamped 200-520px), a main area split into request and response panes by a draggable horizontal divider (ratio clamped 0.15-0.85), and a statusbar. The shell sets theme, accent, and method-style as data-attributes on the document root and exposes empty named mount slots (sidebar, tabs, panes, modals) whose CONTENTS are out of scope. A net-new in-memory zustand settingsStore (mirroring the existing toastStore) is the single source of truth for sidebar width, pane ratio, theme, accent, and method-style; dividers are hand-rolled and clamped, and styling consumes the existing tokens.css contract with semantic class names (the design/reference.html markup is never copied).
current_state: Existing codebase, greenfield for the shell. The renderer root src/renderer/src/App.tsx:10 currently mounts the dev-only PrimitivesDemo gallery (lazy, gated behind import.meta.env.DEV). Feature 001-ui-primitives established the renderer tiers components/atoms + components/molecules + a thin lib/ (cx(), icons-glue, toastStore), and 002-tabs-primitive added the controlled selection-only Tabs molecule; there is NO components/organisms/ tier, no Shell, and no settings store — the only zustand store is src/renderer/src/lib/toastStore.ts. The design-token contract at src/renderer/styles/tokens.css already implements the [data-theme='dark'] override block and the [data-mstyle] method-style variant blocks on <html>, but has NO [data-accent] selector — accent is a single --accent custom property (with --accent-soft/--accent-hover derived via color-mix). Persistence is a main-process responsibility (electron-store appears only in config/docs, not in src); the renderer holds no settings store today. The renderer test stack (docs/architecture.md) is Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event (jsdom) for interaction plus Playwright CT (@playwright/experimental-ct-react) for real-browser focus/keyboard, with tests co-located under __tests__/. The visual/behavior reference is design/reference.html with colors/spacing from design/tokens.json (look/behavior only — never its generated markup).
desired_behavior: A new Shell organism under src/renderer/src/components/organisms/ becomes the App.tsx mount, replacing the PrimitivesDemo root (PrimitivesDemo stays in the repo dev-gated but unmounted). The Shell renders a titlebar (logo, workspace pill, sidebar toggle, command-palette trigger, environment selector, account pill), a resizable left sidebar, a request/response split, and a statusbar. A net-new src/renderer/src/lib/settingsStore.ts zustand store (mirroring toastStore: create(), single module-level instance, typed state + actions, reset for tests, no Node/electron imports) holds { theme, accent, mstyle, sidebarWidth, paneRatio, sidebarCollapsed } IN-MEMORY only and is the single source of truth. The Shell sets data-theme, data-accent, and data-mstyle on the document root (<html>) via an effect from the store, and writes sidebarWidth and paneRatio to CSS custom properties on its own root. A draggable vertical divider resizes the sidebar, clamped to 200-520px; a draggable horizontal divider resizes the request/response split, clamped to a ratio of 0.15-0.85. Dividers are hand-rolled pointer-event components (rAF-batched CSS-var write during drag, store committed on release) exposing the WAI-ARIA separator role with aria-valuenow and keyboard resize, and they handle pointer-release outside the window. Cmd-B toggles a sidebarCollapsed boolean that hides the sidebar entirely while preserving the last sidebarWidth for restore on reopen. When the OS window shrinks below the clamped minimums, the layout re-clamps sidebarWidth (200-520px) and paneRatio (0.15-0.85) so no pane goes negative or overflows. The four named mount slots (sidebar, tabs, panes, modals) accept arbitrary children and render them without the Shell knowing their contents. accent is persisted in the store and set as data-accent, but is visually inert this task (no [data-accent] CSS). Styling uses semantic class names bound to tokens.css with sibling .css files and no inline styles, animations respect prefers-reduced-motion, and behavior (divider drag+clamp, Cmd-B toggle, in-session survival of view settings) is covered by component tests; visual fidelity to design/reference.html is reviewed by design-auditor (never reproducing its markup or cruft).

affected_areas.impact: Create new — net-new components/organisms/ tier holding the shell layout tree (titlebar, resizable sidebar, request/response split, statusbar) + hand-rolled clamped Divider; named mount slots for sidebar/tabs/panes/modals
Create new — zustand store mirroring toastStore; single source of truth for { theme, accent, mstyle, sidebarWidth, paneRatio, sidebarCollapsed } + actions; in-memory only (disk persistence deferred)
Modify — swap the dev-only PrimitivesDemo root for the Shell organism; PrimitivesDemo stays dev-gated (import.meta.env.DEV) but unmounted
Create new — sibling per-component stylesheets using semantic class names bound to tokens.css custom properties; no inline styles; sidebarWidth/paneRatio driven via CSS vars
Reuse (read-only) — shell sets data-theme + data-mstyle on <html> which tokens.css already implements; data-accent is set but visually inert (no [data-accent] block); no edits this task
Create new — Vitest + Testing Library interaction tests and Playwright CT for divider drag/clamp, Cmd-B toggle, in-session setting survival, slot rendering, divider a11y

acceptance_criteria.statement: The renderer shall provide a settingsStore module under the lib directory.
The renderer shall provide a Shell organism component module under the organisms directory.
The renderer source shall contain no reference-export cruft markers.
WHEN the user drags the sidebar divider, the Shell shall resize the sidebar and clamp its width to 200-520px.
WHEN the user presses Cmd-B, the Shell shall toggle the sidebar collapsed state, hiding or showing the sidebar while preserving the last sidebar width for restore on reopen.

WHILE a view setting is held in the settingsStore, the Shell shall reflect data-theme, data-accent, and data-mstyle on the document root and the sidebar width and pane ratio as CSS custom properties for the duration of the session.
WHEN a named mount slot is given children, the Shell shall render those children without depending on their contents.
WHEN App.tsx renders, the Shell shall be the mounted renderer root in place of the PrimitivesDemo gallery.
WHILE a divider has keyboard focus, the Shell shall expose the WAI-ARIA separator role with aria-valuenow and support keyboard resize.
WHEN the accent value changes, the Shell shall set data-accent on the document root, producing no visual change this task.
The exported Shell components, the settingsStore state and actions, and the slot prop types shall carry documentation comments.
The renderer source shall pass strict type-checking.
The codebase shall pass ESLint without new errors.
The Shell component sources shall contain no inline style attributes.
The Shell and settingsStore sources shall not import the electron or node modules directly.
WHEN the user drags the request and response divider, the Shell shall resize the split and keep the pane ratio within its clamped range.
IF the window shrinks below the clamped minimums, THEN the Shell shall re-clamp the sidebar width and the pane ratio to their valid ranges so that no pane is negative or overflows.

ac_subsection_na.behavior_preservation: Greenfield shell — no prior shell/layout behavior to preserve; the App.tsx root swap is a behavior change covered in 5.3, and PrimitivesDemo is dev-only.
ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI pipeline changes in scope; gates run via existing npm scripts, not a new pipeline stage.
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No new git hooks or commit gates introduced by this feature.

out_of_scope.content: Slot CONTENTS — the sidebar tree, tab bodies, request/response editors, and modal bodies that mount into the named slots — are separate downstream tasks; the shell ships empty slots only.

On-disk / electron-store persistence of view settings — the settingsStore is in-memory only this task; disk persistence is deferred to a later persistence-port task (wired through a typed main-process IPC, not the renderer store).
Defining accent palette CSS ([data-accent] selector blocks) — accent is persisted and set as data-accent but is visually inert this task; authoring accent palettes is a later task.
Command-palette overlay + search behavior — the titlebar exposes only a trigger button/slot; the palette itself is out of scope.
Environment selector, workspace pill, and account pill behavior — dropdown data, environment switching, and auth — are out of scope; these are static presentational slots this task.
Multi-window support — the shell is a single-window layout.
Keyboard shortcuts other than Cmd-B — any other shortcut belongs to its own feature.
Reproducing design/reference.html markup or its generated cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel) — reference is for look/behavior only.
Authoring or editing design-token files — the shell consumes src/renderer/styles/tokens.css and design/tokens.json as-is; no token edits.

constraints.content: strict is on; do not weaken it per-file. No 'any'. Type external/IPC input as 'unknown' and narrow with a type guard — the settingsStore state, actions, and slot props are fully typed.
zustand store camelCase + Store suffix (settingsStore); React components PascalCase (Shell, Titlebar, Sidebar, PaneSplit, Statusbar, Divider).
Renderer modules resolve through the @renderer alias; import via the alias, not deep relative paths; keep code in its process dir.

Before writing anything generic/reusable, search for an existing utility — reuse the toastStore zustand pattern, cx(), the Icon atom, and the existing tokens.css contract rather than re-defining them.
Renderer is sandboxed React UI; never import Node or electron directly in renderer code; the settingsStore stays renderer-side and in-memory.

The settingsStore is the single source of truth for sidebar width and pane ratio; drag handlers commit values via store actions, never by mutating state directly (constitution §5 State Management / §4 Always-Do).
Mirror the existing toastStore zustand shape: create(), a single module-level store instance, selector hooks for each field, and a reset for tests so component tests start from known defaults.

Reuse the existing renderer test stack (Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event jsdom for interaction; Playwright CT for real-browser focus/keyboard) under __tests__/; introduce no new test infrastructure.

Style via semantic class names bound to tokens.css custom properties with sibling .css files; drive sidebarWidth/paneRatio through CSS custom properties; no inline styles; animations gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
Implement the WAI-ARIA window-splitter pattern on each divider — role=separator with aria-valuenow/min/max, keyboard resize, and pointer-release-outside-window handling.
Keep contextIsolation intact and the renderer sandboxed — the settingsStore is in-memory only; later disk persistence goes through a typed main-process IPC wrapper, not the renderer store.
Divider drag must stay smooth — no setState-per-pointermove re-render storms; resize is rAF-batched and CSS-var-driven within the frame budget, with the store committed only on pointer release.
Clamp the sidebar width to 200-520px and the request/response pane ratio to 0.15-0.85 in JS, enforced both during drag and on window resize so no pane goes negative or overflows.

open_questions.content: Integration point RESOLVED (Phase 2 fit-check): the user's belief of persisting view settings 'via the existing settings store' is incorrect — no settings store exists; settingsStore is created net-new and in-memory this task, and disk persistence (electron-store/IPC) is deferred to a later persistence-port task.

Divider build-vs-buy is decided as hand-roll (zero-dep, keeps settingsStore as the single source of truth); react-resizable-panels is the documented fallback if the hand-rolled ARIA/keyboard/edge-case work proves more expensive than the derisk spike predicts — final confirmation at /plan.

risks.risk: Hand-rolled divider accessibility (role=separator, aria-valuenow, keyboard resize, pointer-release-outside-window) is more code to get right than a library would give for free.
risks.mitigation: Derisk spike on the divider before locking the approach; axe + keyboard/SR pass; react-resizable-panels documented as the fallback.

risks.risk: Window-resize re-clamp math could yield negative or overflowing panes if the sidebar(200-520px) + pane-ratio(0.15-0.85) bounds are mis-handled on shrink.
risks.mitigation: Prototype the clamp/resize math against the window-shrink edge case; cover with a behavior test.

risks.risk: Drag without rAF/CSS-var batching causes per-pointermove re-render storms and janky resize.
risks.mitigation: rAF-batched CSS-var write during drag; commit to the store only on pointer release; measure vs a setState-per-move baseline.

risks.risk: data-accent is set but visually inert (no [data-accent] CSS), which can look wired-but-broken to a reviewer.
risks.mitigation: Document the inert behavior in the spec/AC; accent palette CSS is an explicit later task.

risks.risk: The named slot API could leak shell knowledge of slot contents, defeating the decoupling goal.
risks.mitigation: Mount the existing Tabs molecule into the tab slot as a content-decoupling proof; slots accept arbitrary children only.

risks.risk: jsdom may not faithfully reproduce pointer-drag and focus fidelity for the dividers, hiding real interaction defects.
risks.mitigation: Run Playwright CT in a real browser for divider drag, keyboard resize, and focus behavior.

approval_summary:
I've created the specification at `specs/003-app-shell-layout/spec.md`. Key points:
- **What changes**: A single-window app shell and layout for the mintEnvoy renderer: a titlebar (logo, workspace pill, sidebar toggle, command-palette trigger, environment selector, account pill), a resizable left sidebar (width clamped 200-520px), a main a...
- **Files affected**: 16 files across 6 areas
- **Acceptance criteria**: 17 testable criteria across 4 AC categories
- **Out of scope**: Slot CONTENTS — the sidebar tree, tab bodies, request/response editors, and moda; On-disk / electron-store persistence of view settings — the settingsStore is in-; Defining accent palette CSS ([data-accent] selector blocks) — accent is persiste; …

Please review and either approve or request changes. Once approved, run `/plan` to create the technical implementation plan.
plan_handoff_block:
## Manual next step — run /plan

A structured handoff (specs/003-app-shell-layout/handoff.json) is written for /plan. /plan auto-discovers it on its first run and reads the upstream plan-seeds — but you still launch /plan manually (there is no auto-dispatch from /specify). Restart Claude Code (exit and relaunch the CLI/app so the newly installed command is picked up), then run the command below in this repo. The spec path is explicit so /plan does not need most-recent-spec discovery:

~~~
/plan specs/003-app-shell-layout/spec.md
~~~
